title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. alpicola
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. alpicola, also known as alpine oxeye daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of alpine oxeye daisy include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been reported to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
